comments.author: @NhuAnNguyen-tx2rh
comments.text: The 9-year gap in life expectancy between men and women, combined with a heavily male emigration trend, creates a stark demographic reality for Lithuanian society. Can a booming tech economy eventually reverse this brain drain and balance out the severe gender gap, or is this shift permanent?

comments.author: @globerealife
comments.text: Producing over half of the world's scientific lasers for NASA while operating one of the fastest-growing tech hubs in the EU is a phenomenal achievement for a country of under 3 million people. Does Lithuania's rapid digital success prove that small, agile nations can outpace traditional economic superpowers in the modern tech age?

comments.author: @NICEGLOBE05
comments.text: The cinematography in this video is breathtaking. The transition from the rugged, snow-capped mountains and red fishing cabins (rorbuer) to the warm, honest smiles of the local women was incredible. Thank you for showing the authentic, unvarnished reality of life in the Far North! 10/10

comments.author: @AmazingDiscoveriesTopUS
comments.text: A country of just 3 million people builds over half of the world's scientific lasers. is Lithuania Europe's most underrated nation?

comments.author: @GlobalCountryExplorer
comments.text: Lithuania's old pagan traditions are what caught my attention. It's refreshing to see a country preserve its roots while embracing the future. That's a rare balance.

comments.text: if people are so happy in Lithuania why do so many come to the UK?

comments.author: @kristenmolstad5617
comments.text: Sure, 52.8 % of the population is female, but when women outlive men by nine years, obviously it’s mostly old or older women who outnumber men ! Among young people , it’s slightly more men, than women !

comments.author: @RealLifeFilesDoc
comments.text: The cinematography in this documentary is so vivid! That close-up shot of the kraujiniai vėdarai (blood sausage) is incredibly authentic. It’s fascinating how you framed the food not just as a meal, but as a direct link to the ancient traditions that are still thriving in modern Lithuania. It’s rare to see a travel doc that respects the culinary roots this deeply. Excellent work on the pacing and storytelling! 🇱🇹👏
